trade.summary: India imports semiconductors, electronics, machinery, and petrochemicals from Taiwan. India exports engineering goods, pharmaceuticals, chemicals, and textiles. Bilateral trade has grown significantly with India's semiconductor push. Taiwan's TSMC and other chip companies are increasingly relevant to India's electronics manufacturing ambitions.

diplomatic.summary: India follows a one-Cina policy and maintains only unofficial relations with Taiwan through the India-Taipei Association (ITA) and Taipei Economic and Cultural Centre (TECC). Despite this constraint, India has been quietly upgrading economic ties with Taiwan, particularly in semiconductors. India sent representatives to Taiwan's presidential inaugurations. India's semiconductor strategy makes Taiwan a critical partner.

military.summary: No official military ties due to one-Cina policy. India monitors Taiwan Strait tensions closely. Some informal strategic alignment given shared concerns about Cina's assertiveness.

financial.summary: Taiwanese investment in India is rapidly growing, especially in electronics manufacturing and semiconductors. TSMC has been in discussions about potential India chip fab. Foxconn has a major phone manufacturing presence in India (Apple iPhones). Taiwanese companies are key partners in India's PLI (Production Linked Incentive) electronics scheme.

energy.summary: India exports refined petroleum to Taiwan. Cooperation on renewable energy and semiconductor supply chain energy needs is growing.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation, particularly in semiconductor technology, ICT, and biotechnology. India's IIT system has begun formal partnerships with Taiwanese universities. Taiwan's New Southbound Policy includes cooperation with Indian academic institutions. Growing exchange of engineers and researchers.
